scope.summary: Seed the missing Satin, Linen, and Spandex system presets into tailor_material_presets so every FabricPreset enum variant has a calibrated is_system_preset=true row, closing the gap between the enum declaration and the seeded library
acceptance_criteria: The tailor_material_presets migration MUST seed Satin (slug='satin'), Linen (slug='linen'), and Spandex (slug='spandex') as is_system_preset=true rows alongside the existing 9 presets

Satin compliance_json MUST reflect low bending stiffness (softer than silk in bending, higher shear than silk), low friction, and buckling_ratio >= 0.85 to match the smooth lustrous drape of satin weave

Linen compliance_json MUST reflect moderate-to-high stretch stiffness (stiffer than cotton, less stiff than canvas), moderate bending stiffness, moderate buckling, and relatively high density (200-300 g/m²)

Spandex physics_json MUST reflect very high stretch compliance (1e-3 range, highest of all presets), very low bending stiffness, and high friction (0.7+ cloth-avatar) matching knit elastic behavior; density approx 130-170 g/m²
All three presets MUST pass the PRESET_NO_NAN, PRESET_STRETCH_NONZERO, PRESET_DENSITY_POS, and PRESET_BBOX_PLAUSIBLE validation checks in the drape test (0.5m x 0.5m panel, 1 second)
FabricPreset::Satin, FabricPreset::Linen, FabricPreset::Spandex MUST each resolve to their system preset row via FabricPreset -> tailor_material_presets slug lookup in the decoder
The fabric integration test MUST assert that silk, satin, and spandex produce measurably different fold-count and bounding-box height at the end of the same 1-second drape test
scope.risk_if_missed: The ContextBundle already advertises satin, linen, spandex as available_presets (spec line ~8600). If the model calls tailor_fabric_preset_create with fabric_archetype='satin' and no system row exists, the decoder will fail or silently fall back to an undefined default, causing an unexplained runtime error that the self-correction loop cannot recover from. The FabricPreset enum and ContextBundle create a false contract with the model that is violated by the missing rows.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
